homepage.title: Hembrug | Danobat
homepage.description: Hembrug, more than 50 years leading hard turning technology. More than 700 high-precision turning solutions developed for customers all around the world.
homepage.text_excerpt:
Hembrug | Danobat
Skip to main content
Hembrug
Hembrug, based in The Netherlands, is the benchmark for fully hydrostatic precision hard turning solutions
Since 1973
Leaders in hard turning. More than 50 years and 700 high-precision turning solutions developed for customers all around the world.
Ultra precision
Hembrug machines are among the most accurate in the world.
Dim. tol. of ≤ 2 µm
Roundness of ≤ 0,5 µm
Ra of 0,1–0,4 µm
> 25 years
Accuracies last more than 25 years. Hembrug offers fully hydrostatic finish hard turning solutions.
Hydrostatic technology enables wear-free operation in the main spindle and slides.
Contact
H. Figeeweg 1a+b
2031 BJ Haarlem
The Netherlands
sales@hembrug.com
| +31 23 51 24 900
service@hembrug.com
| +31 23 51 24 927
Complete complex workpieces with sub-micron tolerances
Hembrug is ideal for machining complex and hardened workpieces in a single set-up and with sub-micron qualities.
You won’t need further post-processing and quality control will be less intensive, cutting your cost.
Hembrug machines address a wide variety of workpieces and materials
Toolholders, dressing tools, machine tool spindle shafts, punches and dies, neck rings, rollers, gear pump shafts, pistons for radial and axial motors, ball screw nuts, and much more.
Hembrug behind the precise movements of humanoid robots
Planetary roller screws enable humanoid robots to perform ultra-precise linear movements — from delicate finger gestures to high-force operations — by using line contact instead of point contact. Hard turning provides a powerful solution for manufacturing these critical components, but achieving sub-micron thread accuracy demands machines far beyond the capabilities of standard CNC lathes. Hembrug holds a decisive technological edge in meeting this challenge.
Finish hard turning: what you did not know yet
This whitepaper explores the fundamentals of finish hard turning. What accuracies can be achieved, how it compares to other techniques such as grinding, what to pay attention to when choosing a suitable machine and what tools are needed among others.
Download >

Our challenges
1679
Founded by the Dutch Government as Artillerie Inrichtingen (A.I.) for the production of ammunition.
Our challenges
1835
After the invention of the steam machine, A.I. started to develop and produce machine tools in-house, such as specific turning lathes, which they needed for ammunition manufacturing.
Our challenges
1940
A.I. started to sell machine tools to other companies for civil purposes.
Our challenges
1969

You won’t need further post-processing and quality control will be less intensive, cutting your cost.
Hembrug machines address a wide variety of workpieces and materials

Hard turning provides a powerful solution for manufacturing these critical components, but achieving sub-micron thread accuracy demands machines far beyond the capabilities of standard CNC lathes. Hembrug holds a decisive technological edge in meeting this challenge.
Finish hard turning: what you did not know yet
This whitepaper explores the fundamentals of finish hard turning. What accuracies can be achieved, how it compares to other techniques such as grinding, what to pay attention to when choosing a suitable machine and what tools are needed among others.

Hybrid horizontal turning and grinding machine
See the machine
Our challenges
1679
Founded by the Dutch Government as Artillerie Inrichtingen (A.I.) for the production of ammunition.
Our challenges
1835
After the invention of the steam machine, A.I. started to develop and produce machine tools in-house, such as specific turning lathes, which they needed for ammunition manufacturing.
Our challenges
1940
A.I. started to sell machine tools to other companies for civil purposes.
Our challenges
1969
The first turning machine with a hydrostatic main spindle for the manufacturing of memory discs, which require a very smooth surface finish.
Our challenges
1982
Hembrug moved to Haarlem and focused only on the manufacturing of high precision, fully hydrostatic turning lathes under the name Mikroturn.
Our challenges
1999
Introduction of the Vertical-Mikroturn series for large workpieces up to a diameter of 1000 mm. This would be the first model from the vertical product range.
Our challenges
2018
Development of the hybrid range with the introduction of the MikroTurnGrind 1000. A full-fledged hybrid hard-turn and fine grinding machine.
Our challenges
2019
Hembrug becomes part of Danobat.
Our challenges
2021
The first MikroTurnGrind vertical: A vertical hard turning and fine grinding machine for large workpieces.
Previous
Next
Would you like to work with us?
Join us
